channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Mexico’s President Sheinbaum threatens to take legal action over the Gulf name change
item.description: Earlier this month, Sheinbaum shared a letter addressed to Google with reporters, arguing that the U.S. had no authority to unilaterally rename the Gulf.
item.dc:creator: Manuela López Restrepo, MPR News

item.title: As sports betting has soared, more people search online for help with gambling addiction
item.description: A study shows more people are looking for help to manage gambling addiction, in the years after a Supreme Court decision allowed online sports betting in 38 states.
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle, MPR News

item.title: Top federal agency exposed for spending billions on migrants in a single year
item.description: The Department of Health and Human Services distributed more than $10 billion in a single year to nonprofits for the care of migrants, according to a new watchdog report.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans consider cuts and work requirements for Medicaid, jeopardizing care for millions
item.description: Republicans are weighing billions of dollars in cuts to Medicaid, jeopardizing health care coverage for some of the 80 million U.S. adults and children enrolled in the safety net program.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Seitz, Andrew Demillo And Kevin Freking, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Gov. Hochul heads to NYC for meetings after 4 deputy mayors resign
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York Gov. Kathy Hochul is holding meetings on Tuesday in New York City as calls grow for Mayor Eric Adams to resign from his position. She’s set to meet with City Comptroller Brad Lander, City Council Speaker Adrienne Adams, Rev.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Senator Brian Jones to propose bill that would reform sanctuary state practices
item.description: Senator Brian Jones is looking to propose a bill that would shake up current legislation that shapes California as a sanctuary state.
item.dc:creator: Kasia Gregorczyk, Fox 5

item.title: House, Senate standoff over Trump agenda escalates
item.description: The showdown between House and Senate Republicans is escalating as the two groups barrel ahead with their contrasting strategies to pass President Trump’s legislative agenda — with the upper chamber expected to move this week.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News Politics Newletter: Duffy Calls Out Clinton
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump right to keep European allies from Ukraine talks, they'd be 'obstacle' to peace, expert says
item.description: President Donald Trump's potential plan to exclude European leaders from negotiations to resolve the war in Ukraine has drawn panic from the continent in recent days.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: European leaders scramble ahead of Trump’s Ukraine summit with Putin
item.description: European leaders were hopeful they could work with the Trump administration on the Ukraine war and other issues. But comments last week by several U.S. officials have poured cold water on such hopes.
item.dc:creator: Eleanor Beardsley, MPR News

item.title: Trump nominates Ed Martin, an ally who defended Jan. 6 rioters, to stay as top DC federal prosecutor
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Conservative activist Ed Martin, who echoed Donald Trump's baseless claims of a stolen 2020 election and defended Jan. 6, 2021, U.S. Capitol rioters, was nominated by the president on Monday to be the top federal prosecutor in Washington on a permanent basis.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats grapple for response on education as GOP charges ahead
item.description: The Democratic Party is having to take a hard look at its education strategy as Republicans move aggressively on the issue with satisfaction in public education at its lowest point in decades.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration tries to bring back fired nuclear weapons workers, reversing DOGE cuts
item.description: he Trump administration has halted the firings of hundreds of federal employees who were tasked with working on the nation's nuclear weapons programs, in an about-face that has left workers confused and experts cautioning that DOGE's blind cost cutting will put communities at risk.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Washington Post backs out of 'Fire Elon Musk' ad order
item.description: The Washington Post this week backed out of a “Fire Elon Musk” advertising order that was to run as a wrap on some of its Tuesday editions, according to the advocacy group Common Cause.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Five pressing questions as RFK Jr. takes HHS reins
item.description: His entire Make America Healthy Again movement is based largely on undermining the scientific and public health messaging from the federal government, along with overhauling the status quo in the food and drug industries.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, Fox 5

item.title: Trump begins firings of FAA staff just weeks after fatal DC plane crash
item.description: The Trump administration has begun firing several hundred Federal Aviation Administration employees, upending staff on a busy air travel weekend and just weeks after a fatal midair collision near Washington.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: To these Black retirees, the federal civil service now under attack was a path to the middle class
item.description: The federal civil service that President Donald Trump and Elon Musk are dismantling has been a way for people of color, in particular Black Americans, to get a toehold into the middle class for decades, as other occupations were closed off because of racial discrimination or patronage.
item.dc:creator: Gary Fields, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US leaders in Saudi Arabia for peace talks on Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said he will not accept a deal without a seat at the negotiating table.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Mike Pence emerges as one of the few Republicans willing to challenge Trump 2.0
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — His group spent nearly $1 million on ads opposing Robert F. Kennedy Jr., Donald Trump's pick to lead the nation's health agencies.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans hope to jam through defense, border funding boost ahead of shutdown fight
item.description: Senate Republicans have the ambitious goal of jamming through funding boosts for defense and border security — separately from the appropriations process — ahead of a mid-March deadline to prevent a government shutdown. Senate Budget Committee Chair Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: Protesters to demand Gov. Hochul remove NYC Mayor Adams from office
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Protesters are expected to descend upon City Hall on Monday to demand Gov. Kathy Hochul remove New York City Mayor Eric Adams from office.
item.dc:creator: Justin Finch, PIX 11

item.title: Ukraine and Europe worry about being sidelined as Trump pushes direct talks with Russia on war's end
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s approach to ending Russia’s war against Ukraine has left European allies and Ukrainian officials worried they are being largely sidelined by the new U.S. administration as Washington and Moscow plan direct negotiations.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Europe tries to muscle up as U.S. plots mediation to help end Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: European leaders are taking steps to answer assertive U.S. efforts to end Russia’s war against Ukraine. France on Sunday announced a meeting of continental leaders and Baltic countries called for new steps to strengthen Kyiv’s hand in any future talks.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Democratic governors diverge on how to handle Trump
item.description: Democratic governors are diverging in their approaches to President Trump amid mounting speculation that some of them are gunning for the White House in 2028. Some are digging their heels into resistance efforts, as in Illinois, where Gov.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fans in Montreal loudly boo U.S. anthem prior to Americans' 4 Nations Face-Off game vs. Canada
item.description: Fans in Montreal loudly booed the U.S. national anthem prior to the 4 Nations Face-Off game between the Americans and host Canada.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: House GOP budget resolution in jeopardy amid concerns from moderates
item.description: The House GOP’s budget resolution could be in jeopardy of not clearing the full chamber amid concerns from moderates over likely cuts to social safety net programs — particularly Medicaid — with a handful undecided on whether they will support the key measure. Reps. David Valadao (R-Calif.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: George has his day, and so does Abe. But states honor US presidents in lots of ways
item.description: TOPEKA, Kan. (AP) — Is Presidents Day the most confusing holiday in the U.S.? States seem to have as many names for it and ideas about whom to honor as there have been presidents. The federal government doesn't even recognize Monday as Presidents Day.
item.dc:creator: JOHN HANNA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Area 51: Fact and fiction
item.description: The Groom Lake, Nevada, facility — about 100 miles northwest of Las Vegas — remains highly secretive.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk shared Mars plan over drinks, Star Trek convo: Entrepreneur
item.description: "I realized he was dead serious. Elon Musk doesn't just dream, he makes things happen," said entrepreneur and academic Vivek Wadhwa.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Zouves, NewsNation

item.title: Here are the agencies purging federal workers
item.description: The moves come as President Trump's Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), led by tech billionaire Elon Musk, have exacerbated their efforts to cut down on government spending — in some cases looking to dismantle whole agencies or departments.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats nervously wait on Trump response to court rulings
item.description: House Democrats howling over the flood of unilateral actions streaming from the White House are voicing confidence that the courts will provide a check on potential abuses of power. They’re less certain, however, about how President Trump will respond.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: German chancellor slams Vance's call for Europe to be inclusive of far-right parties
item.description: Olaf Scholz rebuked U.S. Vice President JD Vance for suggesting that far-right parties should not be shut out of government. Germany's other political parties have refused to work with the far-right AfD.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim and Rob Schmitz, MPR News

item.title: Justice Department fires 20 immigration judges from backlogged courts amid major government cuts
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — The Trump administration fired 20 immigration judges without explanation, a union official said Saturday amid sweeping moves to shrink the size of the federal government.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: House Republicans plot impeachment against judges blocking Trump, DOGE
item.description: Some House Republicans are pledging to bring up impeachment articles against federal judges blocking Trump administration actions.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Ellison tells Minnesota schools to follow law if immigration authorities show up
item.description: Responding to the Trump administration’s decision to allow immigration enforcement in schools, Minnesota Attorney General Keith Ellison on Friday advised district leaders to ensure that they and any federal officials who arrive at schools follow legal protocols.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Shockman, MPR News

item.title: Calls for Mayor Adams’ resignation mount amid turmoil at DOJ
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Calls for Mayor Eric Adams’ resignation are mounting after the Department of Justice ordered his case to be dropped. One call came from Lt. Gov. Antonio Delgado, second-in-command to Gov. Kathy Hochul. Hochul is the only person with the power to remove Adams.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Legislature cancels meetings, closes building as Sierra storm arrives in Nevada
item.description: While Las Vegas drivers navigate rain-soaked roads for the first time in awhile, the Nevada Legislature has shut down for the day to keep drivers out of hazardous conditions in the Reno-Carson City area.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
